hypothesis: Compiling the regex pattern at module level instead of per-call will eliminate pattern compilation overhead on each tokenize() invocation.
change_summary: Move re.compile() to module level as _TOKEN_PATTERN

hypothesis: Walrus operator list comprehension in process_batch eliminates redundant normalize() call per token.
change_summary: Replace for-loop with [n for t in tokens if (n := t.strip().lower())]

hypothesis: sys.intern() on tokens before indexing will deduplicate string objects in memory and speed up dict lookups via identity comparison instead of hash+equality.
change_summary: Wrap each token in sys.intern() before appending to processed list
reason: No improvement. intern() overhead negated any lookup benefit for our stateless per-call pipeline.

hypothesis: collections.Counter is implemented in C and should be faster than a pure-Python dict loop for frequency counting.
change_summary: Replace build_index dict loop with Counter(tokens)
reason: Counter was 11.2% SLOWER than current best (9.8 ms). High unique-token rate (60%) is Counter's worst case. Do not retry.

hypothesis: Caching index.get as a local variable before the loop avoids repeated attribute lookup on each iteration of build_index.
change_summary: Add `index_get = index.get` before the loop; use index_get(token, 0) inside
reason: 4.1% improvement. Small but consistent across all 5 trials.

hypothesis: Distributing token processing across worker processes using multiprocessing.Pool will parallelize the CPU-bound work.
change_summary: Wrap run_pipeline in multiprocessing.Pool with 4 workers, split token list into chunks per worker
reason: Process spawn overhead (>50 ms) completely dominates the 9 ms baseline. Only viable for inputs >50k tokens.

hypothesis: numpy vectorized operations for token frequency counting should be faster than a Python dict loop.
change_summary: Convert token list to numpy array, use np.unique with return_counts=True
reason: Conversion overhead to numpy array exceeded counting benefit. Python→numpy→Python round-trip is expensive for <10k tokens.

hypothesis: Processing tokens in fixed-size chunks will improve CPU cache utilization by keeping the working set (token list slice + partial index) within L1 cache bounds, reducing cache misses in build_index.
change_summary: Introduce chunked_pipeline() that splits all_tokens into CHUNK_SIZE=512 windows, processes+counts each chunk, then merges
reason: 9.6% improvement. Tested 128/256/512/1024: 512 was best. 1024 showed cache thrash. Keep CHUNK_SIZE=512.

hypothesis: Pre-allocating the result dict with an estimated token count will reduce rehashing overhead.
change_summary: Initialize index = dict.fromkeys(tokens, 0) before counting loop
reason: No improvement. CPython dict pre-allocation doesn't work as expected — fromkeys() iterates twice and Python dicts handle amortized growth efficiently already.

hypothesis: Try Cython-compiled inner loop for build_index to eliminate Python interpreter overhead per iteration.
change_summary: Build Cython extension for the counting loop
reason: 16.5% improvement. Requires Cython build step but tests pass. Best result so far: 7.1 ms (43% from baseline).
